title: Please correct the following issues and upload a new binary to App Store Connect.
author: Search26
body:
ITMS-91054: Invalid API category declaration - The PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy file at the “TLPhotoPicker_TLPhotoPicker.bundle/PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy” path contains “NSPrivacyAccessedAPICategoryPhotoLibrary” as the value for a NSPrivacyAccessedAPIType key, which is invalid. Values for NSPrivacyAccessedAPIType keys in your app’s privacy manifests must be valid API categories. For more details about this policy, including a list of required reason APIs and approved reasons for usage, visit

does anyone facing the same issue like that when upload app to appstore.

title: PrivacyInfo - ITMS-91054
author: marinofaggiana
body:
Hello,

We noticed one or more issues with a recent submission for TestFlight review for the following app:

xxxxx
App Apple ID xxxxx
Version xxxxx
Build x
Please correct the following issues and upload a new binary to App Store Connect.

ITMS-91054: Invalid API category declaration - The PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy file at the “TLPhotoPicker_TLPhotoPicker.bundle/PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy” path contains “NSPrivacyAccessedAPICategoryPhotoLibrary” as the value for a NSPrivacyAccessedAPIType key, which is invalid. Values for NSPrivacyAccessedAPIType keys in your app’s privacy manifests must be valid API categories. For more details about this policy, including a list of required reason APIs and approved reasons for usage, visit: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/bundleresources/privacy_manifest_files/describing_use_of_required_reason_api.

Apple Developer Relations

title: EmptyView is displayed incorrectly
author: dev4jam
body:
EmptyView is visible when it shouldn't. 

Use case:
1. Open picker and give limited access to photo library
2. Select several photos and close image selection
3. Open picker and select "Add more photos" near Done button
4. Add more photos and select Done
5. Select "Add more photos" near Done button
6. Deselect everything and select Done button
7. At this state you should be able to see only camera tile
8. Close picker
9. Open picker. You should see EmptyView
10. Select "Add more photos" near Done button
11. Add some photos and select Done
12. EmptyView is still displayed and block collection view with available photos

title: 'shared' is unavailable in application extensions for iOS: Use view controller based solutions where appropriate instead.
author: sagarthecoder
body:
Got this error (`'shared' is unavailable in application extensions for iOS: Use view controller based solutions where appropriate instead.`) inside `TLAssetPreviewViewController` class and in this line
`updatePreferredContentSize(for: asset, isPortrait: UIApplication.shared.orientation?.isPortrait == true)`
When I'm using this pods in any App Extension. Though I solved this by following ways.
1. go to `Pods` project
2. Select `TLPhotoPicker`
3. Build Setting
4. set `NO` for `Require Only App-Extension-Safe-API`
But Can you solve this please. Because when I install pod again then everytime I need to solve this error manually.

title: Any options for showing GIFs which has fixed loops?
author: sagarthecoder
body:
I can access and show all GIFS by using following codes
`configure.mediaType = .image`
`configure.fetchCollectionTypes = [(PHAssetCollectionType.smartAlbum, PHAssetCollectionSubtype.smartAlbumAnimated)]`
But I want to show gifs which has fixed loops (not repeating always). Is it possible using TLPhotoPicker?

title: Album sort order
author: ripplek
body: How can I specify `PHAssetCollection.localizedTitle` ahead of collections, or `focusFirstCollection` to the `localizedTitle`?

title: `tempCopyMediaFile` will overwrite tmp images with same originalFileName.
author: ouabing
body:
Currently `tempCopyMediaFile` will use `asset.originalFileName` as the default tmp filename. Files which have same names will be overwritten when calling `tempCopyMediaFile` in a loop.

What about providing an option to customize the tmp file name?
